quizQuestion:
Espero que {___} bien.
(I hope you arrived safely.)
explanation: hayas llegado — present perfect subjunctive (pretérito perfecto de subjuntivo). Formation: haya/hayas/haya/hayamos/hayáis/hayan + past participle. After espero que (present hope about a completed action), use present perfect subjunctive.

quizQuestion:
No creo que {___} ya.
(I don't think he has finished yet.)
sentenceTranslation: I don't think he has finished yet.
explanation: haya terminado — present perfect subjunctive. After no creo que (doubt trigger in present tense), use present perfect subjunctive for a completed action. The él/ella form of haber in present subjunctive is haya.

quizQuestion:
Es posible que {___} ya.
(It's possible they have already left.)
sentenceTranslation: It's possible they have already left.
explanation: hayan salido — present perfect subjunctive. After es posible que (impersonal trigger), use present perfect subjunctive. Ellos/ellas form of haber in present subjunctive: hayan.

quizQuestion:
Me alegra que {___}.
(I'm glad you have come.)
explanation: hayas venido — present perfect subjunctive. After me alegra que (present emotion about a completed action), use present perfect subjunctive. Venido is the irregular past participle of venir.

quizQuestion:
Cuando {___}, llámame.
(When you have eaten, call me.)
sentenceTranslation: When you have eaten, call me.
grammarPointLabel: present perfect subjunctive: hayas + comido — cuando (temporal)
explanation: hayas comido — present perfect subjunctive after cuando in a future temporal clause. When the action must be completed before something else happens, use present perfect subjunctive: cuando + hayas + past participle.

quizQuestion:
Dudo que {___} eso en serio.
(I doubt he said that seriously.)
sentenceTranslation: I doubt he said that seriously.
grammarPointLabel: present perfect subjunctive: haya + dicho (él/ella) — doubt
explanation: haya dicho — present perfect subjunctive. After dudo que (doubt trigger), use present perfect subjunctive for a completed action. Dicho is the irregular past participle of decir.

quizQuestion:
Es importante que {___} para entonces.
(It's important that we have finished by then.)
sentenceTranslation: It's important that we have finished by then.
explanation: hayamos terminado — present perfect subjunctive, nosotros form. After es importante que, use present perfect subjunctive to show that the action must be completed by a future point. Hayamos is the nosotros form of haber in present subjunctive.

quizQuestion:
Espero que {___} a tiempo.
(I hope she arrived on time.)
sentenceTranslation: I hope she arrived on time.
explanation: haya llegado — present perfect subjunctive, él/ella form. After espero que (present hope about a completed action), use present perfect subjunctive. The yo/él form of haber in present subjunctive is haya.

quizQuestion:
No creo que {___} un error.
(I don't think we have made a mistake.)
sentenceTranslation: I don't think we have made a mistake.
grammarPointLabel: present perfect subjunctive: hayamos + cometido (nosotros) — doubt
explanation: hayamos cometido — present perfect subjunctive, nosotros form. After no creo que (doubt about a completed action), use present perfect subjunctive. Hayamos = nosotros form of haber in present subjunctive.

quizQuestion:
Si {___} más, habría aprobado.
(If I had studied more, I would have passed.)
sentenceTranslation: If I had studied more, I would have passed.
grammarPointLabel: pluperfect subjunctive: hubiera + estudiado — si-clause type 3
explanation: hubiera estudiado — pluperfect subjunctive (pluscuamperfecto de subjuntivo). Formation: hubiera/hubiese + past participle. This is the if-clause of a type 3 (counterfactual past) conditional. Key B2 structure.

quizQuestion:
Me alegró que {___} a la reunión.
(I was glad he had come to the meeting.)
sentenceTranslation: I was glad he had come to the meeting.
grammarPointLabel: pluperfect subjunctive: hubiera + venido (él/ella) — past emotion
explanation: hubiera venido — pluperfect subjunctive. After me alegró que (past emotion + completed action), use pluperfect subjunctive. The yo/él form of haber in imperfect subjunctive is hubiera.

quizQuestion:
No creía que {___} ya.
(I didn't think they had already finished.)
sentenceTranslation: I didn't think they had already finished.
grammarPointLabel: pluperfect subjunctive: hubieran + terminado (ellos/ellas) — past doubt
explanation: hubieran terminado — pluperfect subjunctive. After no creía que (past doubt about a completed action), use pluperfect subjunctive. Hubieran is the ellos/ellas form.

quizQuestion:
Si {___} antes, habrías visto la función.
(If you had arrived earlier, you would have seen the show.)
sentenceTranslation: If you had arrived earlier, you would have seen the show.
grammarPointLabel: pluperfect subjunctive: hubieras + llegado (tú) — si-clause type 3
explanation: hubieras llegado — pluperfect subjunctive, tú form. Si-clause type 3: si + pluperfect subj + conditional perfect. Hubieras is the tú form of hubiera (imperfect subjunctive of haber).

quizQuestion:
Si lo {___}, no habría ido.
(If I had known, I would not have gone.)
sentenceTranslation: If I had known, I would not have gone.
grammarPointLabel: pluperfect subjunctive: hubiera + sabido — si-clause type 3
explanation: hubiera sabido — pluperfect subjunctive, yo form. Another type 3 si-clause: si + pluperfect subj (hubiera sabido) + conditional perfect (no habría ido). Sabido is the past participle of saber.

quizQuestion:
Habla como si {___} en Madrid siempre.
(He speaks as if he had always lived in Madrid.)
sentenceTranslation: He speaks as if he had always lived in Madrid.
grammarPointLabel: pluperfect subjunctive: hubiera + vivido — como si (as if)
explanation: hubiera vivido — pluperfect subjunctive after como si (as if). Como si always takes subjunctive: imperfect subjunctive for present comparisons, pluperfect subjunctive for past comparisons.

quizQuestion:
Si {___} antes, habrían visto todo.
(If they had arrived earlier, they would have seen everything.)
sentenceTranslation: If they had arrived earlier, they would have seen everything.
grammarPointLabel: pluperfect subjunctive: hubieran + llegado (ellos/ellas) — si-clause type 3
explanation: hubieran llegado — pluperfect subjunctive, ellos/ellas form. Type 3 si-clause: si + pluperfect subj (hubieran llegado) + conditional perfect (habrían visto). Hubieran = ellos/ellas form.
